sentences.en: Monday. Today was the formal class debate Mr. Pan promised us weeks ago, and the topic turned out to be very personal: should students be allowed to use phones during lunch break?
sentences.zh: 星期一。今天是潘老師好幾週前就答應我們的正式班級辯論，題目結果非常切身：學生午休時間該不該准許使用手機？

sentences.en: Bernice and I were placed on the same side, arguing in favor, while our classmate Derek led the opposing view.
sentences.zh: Bernice 和我被分在同一邊，主張贊成，而我們同學 Derek 則帶領反對的那一方。

sentences.en: "A good debate is not about who disagrees the loudest," Mr. Pan reminded us before we began. "It is about clear reasons, not raised voices."
sentences.zh: 「一場好的辯論，不是看誰反對得最大聲，」開始前潘老師提醒我們：「而是看誰的理由最清楚，而不是聲音最大。」

sentences.en: I started by stating our opinion clearly: a short break with phones allowed could help students relax before afternoon classes.
sentences.zh: 我一開始就清楚陳述我們的立場：短暫的休息時間准許用手機，能幫助學生在下午的課之前放鬆一下。

sentences.en: Derek stood up to respond, expressing his own view firmly: phones during lunch would only lead to more arguments and less actual talking between friends.
sentences.zh: Derek 站起來回應，堅定地表達了他自己的看法：午休用手機只會帶來更多爭執，朋友之間真正的交談反而變少。

sentences.en: "I do not deny that phones can be useful," he said, "but I accept the idea that lunch break should stay a phone-free space for real conversation."
sentences.zh: 他說：「我不否認手機很有用，」「但我認同午休應該保留成一個不用手機、能真正交談的時間空間。」

sentences.en: Bernice replied quickly, arguing that some students use their lunch break to message parents about after-school plans, not just to scroll aimlessly.
sentences.zh: Bernice 很快回應，主張有些學生用午休時間傳訊息給家長討論放學後的計畫，而不是漫無目的地滑手機。

sentences.en: One classmate acting as a neutral speaker read a short prepared statement, listing both sides' strongest points fairly.
sentences.zh: 一位扮演中立發言人角色的同學，唸出一份事先準備好的簡短聲明，公平列出雙方最有力的論點。

sentences.en: The rest of the class acted as listeners, occasionally nodding, occasionally whispering their own opinion to the person sitting next to them.
sentences.zh: 班上其他人則擔任聽眾，時不時點頭，時不時小聲把自己的意見講給旁邊的人聽。

sentences.en: Derek's team tried to prove their point using an example: a real incident last year when a phone disagreement turned into a bigger argument between two students.
sentences.zh: Derek 那組試圖用一個例子來證明他們的論點：去年一次因手機而起的爭執，最後演變成兩位學生之間更大的衝突。

sentences.en: I responded that one bad incident does not necessarily indicate that every student would misuse the privilege.
sentences.zh: 我回應說，一個不好的例子，並不能就此表明每個學生都會濫用這項權利。

sentences.en: Near the end, Mr. Pan asked both sides to conclude with one final, clear statement summarizing their whole argument.
sentences.zh: 接近尾聲時，潘老師要求雙方都用一句最後、清楚的結論總結他們整個論點。

sentences.en: "Trust students with small freedoms," I concluded, "and most of them will prove they deserve bigger ones later."
sentences.zh: 我做結論：「相信學生擁有小小的自由，」「大部分人之後都會證明自己配得上更大的自由。」

sentences.en: Derek concluded with his own strong statement: "Some rules protect the exact thing we say we want: real connection between real people."
sentences.zh: Derek 也用他自己有力的結論收尾：「有些規則保護的，正是我們口口聲聲說想要的東西：人與人之間真正的連結。」

sentences.en: Mr. Pan then let the whole class vote, and the result was surprisingly close, only two votes separating the two opinions.
sentences.zh: 潘老師接著讓全班投票，結果出乎意料地接近，兩種意見之間只差了兩票。

sentences.en: "This is exactly why real policies take time," he said, smiling at both sides. "Neither view is wrong; you both expressed your reasons well."
sentences.zh: 他對兩邊都笑了笑說：「這正是為什麼真正的政策需要時間，」「哪一種看法都不算錯，你們兩邊都把理由表達得很好。」

sentences.en: Bernice admitted afterward that hearing Derek's side actually changed her opinion slightly, even though she would never openly admit that to him.
sentences.zh: 事後 Bernice 坦白說，聽了 Derek 那邊的論點，她的想法其實稍微改變了，雖然她絕對不會公開跟他承認這件事。

sentences.en: Lesson of the day: a good debate does not need a clear winner to feel like a genuine success.
sentences.zh: 今日心得：一場好的辯論，不需要一個明確的贏家，也能感覺像一場真正的成功。

quiz.question: What is the debate topic in class today?
options: Whether students should be allowed to use phones during lunch break.

quiz.question: What does Mr. Pan say makes a good debate?
options: Whoever has the most friends supporting them.

quiz.question: How close is the final class vote?
options: Only two votes separate the two opinions.
Derek's side wins by a landslide.
